sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: 1. FC Union Berlin, sitting at 13th in the league with 37 points, hosts 1. FC Heidenheim, who are struggling in 16th place with only 26 points. This matchup at An der alten Försterei in Berlin promises to be a crucial encounter for both sides. Union Berlin will look to capitalize on their home advantage, while Heidenheim aim to climb out of the relegation zone. The referee, Florian Badstubner, averages 3.8 yellow cards per game, which could influence the physicality of this contest. With Union's higher league position, they enter as slight favorites, but Heidenheim's desperation for points cannot be underestimated.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these teams, 1. FC Heidenheim holds a surprising edge with 3 wins compared to Union Berlin's 1 win and 1 draw. Their most recent clash on January 11, 2025, saw Heidenheim triumph 2-0 at home, with goalkeeper Kevin Müller earning an impressive 8.3 Sofascore rating as the player of the match. Head-to-head trends show Heidenheim being the first to score in 8 of their last 10 encounters and maintaining an unbeaten streak of 3 games against Union. Additionally, matches between these sides often see less than 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 7 meetings, hinting at a potentially low-scoring affair.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Union Berlin are on an impressive streak of 8 games without a loss, though they’ve failed to win in their last 4 outings. They’ve also struggled defensively at home, failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 3 matches, with both teams scoring in 6 of their last 8 home games. On the other hand, Heidenheim’s away form shows discipline in avoiding excessive bookings, with less than 4.5 cards in their last 5 away games. Both teams have a tendency for low corner counts, with Union Berlin and Heidenheim seeing under 10.5 corners in 5 of 6 and 8 of 9 recent games, respectively. This suggests a game that might lack expansive, attacking play.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Union Berlin
sections.text: For Union Berlin, forward Benedict Hollerbach stands out with 8 goals this season, overperforming his expected goals (xG) of 5.84 by a significant 2.16. Defender Leopold Querfeld, ranked 1st in the league for percentage of long balls in total passes at 11.4%, is another player to watch, especially with his recent form averaging a 7.54 Sofascore rating over the last 5 matches, including 1 goal and 2 assists. Additionally, Andrej Ilić has contributed 5 goals, showing promise upfront despite no assists. Midfielder Tom Rothe also adds balance with 3 goals and 3 assists, making Union’s attack multifaceted.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Heidenheim
sections.text: Heidenheim’s attack is led by forward Marvin Pieringer, who has scored 7 goals despite an xG of 8.59, showing he’s a clinical finisher with 2 assists to his name. Midfielder Mathias Honsak has also been effective with 5 goals, overperforming his xG of 3.22 by 1.78. Notably, Leo Scienza, ranked 3rd in the league for fouls drawn per game at 2.4, could be a key asset in winning set pieces, though he is currently listed as a missing player for this match. Defender Patrick Mainka, with a standout average Sofascore rating of 7.32 across the last 5 matchups against Union, remains a defensive rock.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Style
sections.text: Union Berlin have scored 33 goals this season, ranking 15th in the league, but they’ve overperformed their expected goals by 0.62, placing them 4th in xG overperformance. Their defense, conceding 47 goals, ranks 8th for least goals conceded, while their 8 clean sheets place them 10th. However, their possession stats are poor at 39.29%, ranking 18th, alongside a low 74.44% pass accuracy. Heidenheim’s goal distribution shows a knack for scoring late, with 20% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Union, meanwhile, score often early, with 21.74% of goals in the first 15 minutes, suggesting contrasting game tempos.

sections.subtitle: Injuries and Absences
sections.text: Union Berlin will miss key players like defender Diogo Leite, who has a strong 7.18 average rating, and midfielder Woo-Yeong Jeong, with 3 goals and 2 assists. These absences could affect their defensive stability and midfield creativity. Heidenheim, on the other hand, will be without Leo Scienza, a significant loss given his ability to draw fouls and contribute offensively with 3 goals and 2 assists. Forward Sirlord Conteh’s absence further weakens their attacking options. Both teams will need to rely on squad depth to compensate for these gaps.
